name: Woody Guthrie
abstract: Woodrow Wilson Guthrie (; July 14, 1912 – October 3, 1967) was an American singer, songwriter,  and composer widely considered one of the most significant figures in American folk music. His work focused on themes of American socialism and anti-fascism and has inspired many generations politically and musically with songs such as "This L…

The Biggest Thing That Man Has Ever Done

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: You could say that one of the things that left-wingers achieved culturally in this country, they made a big contribution to American folk music. There’s Woody Guthrie and Pete Seeger and all that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t know his name. I didn’t think he was much of a guitarist... There was something about him, though.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
